macro.title: Senate Democrats introduce bill to automatically fund CFPB
macro.what: Senate Democrats introduced a bill to automatically fund the CFPB via Fed transfers, aiming to limit future cuts and pressure.
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

macro.title: UWM sanctioned after judge orders Ishbia deposition
macro.what: A federal judge has ordered United Wholesale Mortgage (UWM) to make its CEO, Mat Ishbia, available for a deposition in a dispute with Atlantic Trust Mortgage Corporation.
macro.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

macro.title: Strong jobs data, inflation concerns keep mortgage rates elevated
macro.what: Conforming averaged 6.78% and FHA 6.33% as strong jobs data and inflation kept bond yields elevated and rates sticky.

macro.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

macro.title: This Week’s Inflation and Sentiment Data May Clarify the Fed’s Path
macro.what: This Week In A Nutshell: With the Strait of Hormuz still closed and fresh off a hot jobs report last week, rates are at risk of climbing further this week if Wednesday’s inflation data comes in above expectations

housing.title: IBHS adds neighborhood and multifamily wildfire standards for Western builders
housing.what: The Insurance Institute for Business & Home Safety (IBHS) has expanded its Wildfire Prepared program with new standards for neighborhoods and multifamily buildings and updated requirements for single-family homes, creating a more complete design roadmap for builders in fire-prone communities
housing.why: Supply pipeline sets future inventory and construction activity.
housing.market: Stronger pipeline aids suppliers; oversupply later can weigh on pricing.

housing.title: Existing home sales beat estimates, what it signals for 2026
housing.what: NAR existing home sales rose 3.2% in May and the Housing Market Tracker has shown improved demand since late March.
housing.why: Relevant development for housing and markets.
housing.market: Watch second-order effects across builders, lenders, and REITs.

housing.title: Mortgage credit availability edges higher in May
housing.what: Mortgage credit availability ticked up 0.1% in May as jumbo programs loosened slightly and government and conforming credit held steady.
housing.why: Rates & inflation shape discount rates and housing affordability.
housing.market: Lower rates support demand/valuations; higher rates pressure builders/REITs and slow transactions.

housing.title: Existing home sales rise 3.2% in May to 4.17 million
housing.what: Existing home sales rose 3.2% in May to 4.17 million, inventory hit 1.55 million and the median price climbed 1.3% to $429,300.
housing.why: Price trend signals wealth effects and buyer/seller power.
housing.market: Firm prices help builder margins; softness hits comps and sentiment.
